entries.authority_rule: Do not merge. Constitutional text governs system priority order; philosophy manifesto frames public ethical stance.
entries.editing_rule: Cross-link and scope-label both files instead of deduping by basename.
entries.rationale: Same basename but different semantic layer: one is a constitutional rule surface, the other is a philosophical declaration.

roles.docs/philosophy/academic_grounding.md: public academic grounding and citation surface
roles.memory/narrative/threads/academic_ground: private narrative thread and evolving meaning-transfer note

entries.authority_rule: Do not sync by content. Public philosophy docs and private memory threads serve different retrieval audiences.
entries.editing_rule: Only edit the public philosophy surface in this repository cleanup lane; do not treat the memory thread as a mirror.
entries.rationale: One file is a public scholarly grounding document; the other is a private narrative thread capturing evolving framing.

entries.authority_rule: These are separate generated lanes and must stay namespaced by directory, not deduped by basename.
entries.editing_rule: Do not hand-edit generated artifacts. If naming changes are needed, change generator output names later.
entries.rationale: Same basename arises from two runtime namespaces, not from accidental duplication.

entries.authority_rule: Backend metadata files are lane-specific. Treat them as separate runtime stores, not as mirrors.
entries.editing_rule: Do not normalize or dedupe these files during public doc cleanup.
entries.rationale: The same filename reflects backend-local metadata conventions rather than shared content.

roles.memory/memory/.hierarchical_index/vows_m: nested legacy or restored shadow copy

entries.authority_rule: Do not modify during public cleanup. Govern this pair through the dedicated private-memory shadow boundary contract until a later cleanup pass is explicitly in scope.

entries.rationale: This pair looks like a legacy or restored nested shadow rather than an intentional mirror, but it sits inside protected memory territory.
